shape: Hd=128 Hq=32 Hkv=8 G=4 ctx=1024, gqa_coop_vec vs llama flash_attn_tile

ruled_out.occupancy: REFUTED — all tinygrad kernels at 100% theoretical occupancy (VGPR-limited 16 waves/SIMD, LDS=0)
ruled_out.registers_spills: REFUTED — flash_partial VGPR=13, 0 scratch, 0 vspill; no kernel spills
ruled_out.fundamental_hw_limit: REFUTED — llama hits 12.2us on the SAME hardware; the hardware/algorithm allows it

root_cause.primary_codegen: tinygrad emits SCALAR, un-tiled code for the PV/softmax-weighting (flash_partial): 0 v_dot2_f32_f16, 0 LDS, scalar fp16 V loads (global_load_d16_b16, 2/iter), 7 fma/dot total. Standalone 201 GFLOPS / 60 GB/s -> neither compute- nor bandwidth-saturated = latency/issue-bound. llama's tile does the SAME work with 1024 v_dot2_f32_f16 + 1056 v_pk + 268 ds_read of LDS-staged K/V, fused. The q.k MATMUL (LDS=64, tiled) IS fast (13.9us ~= llama whole tile) BECAUSE tinygrad's tiled-GEMM codegen applies to matmul-shaped ops; flash_partial is a hand-rolled reduction (LDS=0) so it does NOT get tiled/packed.
root_cause.secondary_fragmentation: 6 separate kernels + matmul, each a launch + HBM round-trip of scores/pm/prob/pout/gm/den. The LSE reduces (flash_gmax/den/combine, VGPR 2-6) run as ~1-thread-wide workgroups over Hq=32 -> near-empty GPU paying launch+sync latency. llama fuses all into one tile (9.16us) + combine (3.08us).

fixable_lever.named: Get tinygrad to emit LDS-tiled, packed-fp16 (v_dot2_f32_f16) code for the dominant PV/softmax-weighting (flash_partial). The hand-rolled scalar reduction is the inefficiency; the matmul codegen path is the fast one.
fixable_lever.most_bounded_first_step: Express the PV weighting as a tinygrad MATMUL (prob @ V) so the EXISTING tiled-GEMM codegen (which makes the q.k matmul fast at 13.9us) applies, instead of the scalar flash_partial. Estimated: flash_partial 24.7us -> ~14us (q.k-matmul-class) => coop ~70us -> ~60us ~ 1.16x standalone attention.
fixable_lever.full_fix: A single LDS-tiled fused flash kernel with v_dot2/packed-fp16 (the llama structure) = the DEEP codegen capability (tinygrad's UOp codegen does not emit LDS-tiled flash reductions; fusion blocked by the Q8L-2 two-granularity store wall + Path A per-lane exp redundancy). Multi-week.
fixable_lever.ev_caveat: The bounded matmul-PV step is ~1.16x standalone attention; attention is ~23% of decode -> ~3-4% whole-decode, likely BELOW the 5% W==D promotion bar (LOCAL_PASS_WD_FAIL class, like FLASH_L=64). The full llama-class win needs the deep fused tiled-codegen capability.
fixable_lever.not_a_closed_lane: This is NOT the closed 'coop-qk-preserving redesign' (that was closed on TIMING -- 'combine ~1us, no delta' -- without ISA attribution). The ISA now shows a SPECIFIC fixable codegen inefficiency in the PV partial (24.7us scalar). NEW evidence.

counter_reliability.rocprofv3_pmc_llama: PARTIAL — SQ_WAVES, SQ_BUSY_CYCLES real; SQ_INSTS_VALU/LDS, GRBM_GUI_ACTIVE return 0 (unsupported gfx1100+rocprofv3-7.2.4)
counter_reliability.rocprofv3_tinygrad: FAILS — rocprofv3 does not hook tinygrad's HCQ/direct-ring dispatch (zero output)
counter_reliability.reliable_signals_used: ISA disasm (llvm-objdump), kernel-descriptor resources (VGPR/SGPR/LDS/scratch/spill), theoretical occupancy, tinygrad ProfileGraphEvent durations + GFLOPS/GBs. Sufficient to attribute WITHOUT live VALU/cache counters.

next_scoped_action: Scope (NOT build in attribution): a bounded diagnostic measuring a matmul-PV / tiled-PV decode attention vs gqa_coop_vec @ctx512/1024/4096 (does routing the PV through the tiled-GEMM codegen recover the ~10us). First gate local A/B; W==D only if local passes; expect LOCAL_PASS_WD_FAIL-class. If the bounded step is W==D-marginal AND the full LDS-tiled-fused-flash codegen capability is not funded -> REST_DECODE with this counter-level evidence.
